@uncledullaz / day 44 — react state, events & functions

> how to export:
  1. Wait 2–3s for Google Fonts to load, then click download all 9 slides.
  2. Top-corner logos load from ../google-keep-logo.png and ../react-logo.svg.png (parent folder). Serve day-44/ over http://localhost so exports can inline them; file:// may fail on slides with those paths.
  3. Upload all 9 slides in order to Instagram as a carousel.

03/09
~/day44$cat learnings.log
[ DAY 44 ] REACT FUNDAMENTALS

What I learnt.

// 4 core concepts covered today

01
Arrow functions vs regular syntax differences · this is lexically scoped in arrows · dynamic in regular functions
02
Event handling in React camelCase events: onClick, onChange · pass handler reference, not a call · receives SyntheticEvent
03
State & useState useState(init) returns [value, setter] · calling setter triggers a re-render
04
Two-way binding onChange updates state from UI · value prop reads state back → always in sync
[PROGRESS] day 44·zaio bootcamp | react
04/09
~/day44$cat functions.js
[ CONCEPT ] ARROW VS REGULAR FUNCTIONS

this is the difference.

// regular function — this bound to caller function greet(name) { console.log(this); // depends on caller return "Hello, " + name; } // arrow function — this from outer scope const greet = (name) => { console.log(this); // lexical — outer scope return "Hello, " + name; } // concise single-expression form const double = (n) => n * 2; // in React — always arrow for handlers const handleClick = () => setTitle("clicked");
rule: use arrows for callbacks & handlers
05/09
~/day44$cat Note.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] EVENT HANDLING IN REACT

Events in JSX.

// camelCase — not onclick, but onClick const Note = (props) => { const [title, setTitle] = useState(props.title); // handler — defined as arrow function const noteClickHandler = () => { setTitle("new title"); }; // pass reference, not a call — no () return ( <div onClick={noteClickHandler}> {title} </div> ); };
key: onClick={fn} not onClick={fn()}
06/09
~/day44$man useState

useState

// react’s hook for local component state

import { useState } from "react"; // syntax: const [value, setter] = useState(initial) const [title, setTitle] = useState(""); const [text, setText] = useState(""); // calling setter schedules a re-render const handleChange = (e) => setTitle(e.target.value); // on submit — reset fields const handleSubmit = (e) => { e.preventDefault(); setTitle(""); setText(""); };
value — current state, read-only setter — updates value & triggers re-render initial — useState("") / useState(0) / useState([])
07/09
~/day44$cat Form.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] TWO-WAY BINDING

State ↔ UI.

// Form.jsx — input wired to state const [title, setTitle] = useState(""); // UI → State: every keystroke updates state const handleTitle = (e) => setTitle(e.target.value); // State → UI: value prop reads from state <input onChange={handleTitle} // UI → State value={title} // State → UI placeholder="Title" /> // submit resets: input clears automatically const handleSubmit = (e) => { e.preventDefault(); setTitle(""); };
pattern: controlled component·state is single source of truth
08/09
